description: SalesBlock.io — Sales Productivity Cockpit. Timed, calendar-synced focus sessions with curated contact lists for sales reps.

userStories.title: Supabase project init and org/user schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Supabase initialized with auth and the core org/user tables so all features have a foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Supabase project initialized with email+password auth enabled
SQL migration creates organizations table: id, name, logo_url, settings (jsonb), stripe_customer_id, created_at
SQL migration creates divisions table: id, org_id (FK), name, created_at
SQL migration creates teams table: id, org_id (FK), division_id (FK nullable), name, created_at
SQL migration creates users table: id (auth.users FK), org_id (FK), team_id (FK), role (enum: sdr, ae, manager), display_name, email, preferences (jsonb), created_at
Foreign key constraints and indexes on all FK columns
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: RLS policies for org/user tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need row-level security policies so users only see their own org's data and managers see their team.
acceptanceCriteria: RLS enabled on organizations, divisions, teams, users tables
Users can only read/write their own org's data
Managers see their team's data
Higher roles see cascading data (division heads see all teams in division)
Policies tested with at least 2 different user roles
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Vite + React + TypeScript project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the frontend project initialized with the correct tech stack so development can begin.
acceptanceCriteria: Vite + React + TypeScript project created with correct tsconfig
Tailwind CSS configured with dark mode class strategy
Lucide React icons installed
Supabase JS client installed and configured with env vars
React Router v6 installed with basic route structure
TanStack Query installed and provider set up
App renders without errors at localhost
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Authentication flow: sign up, sign in, sign out
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create an account, sign in, and sign out so I can access my workspace securely.
acceptanceCriteria: Sign-up page: email, password, display name, organization name (creates new org + user as manager)
Sign-in page: email + password with Forgot password link
After sign-in, redirect to home dashboard route
Sign-out clears session and redirects to sign-in
Auth state persisted via Supabase session with auto-refresh tokens
Protected route wrapper redirects unauthenticated users to sign-in
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: App layout with sidebar navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a persistent sidebar navigation so I can quickly access all areas of the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar with items: Home, SalesBlocks, Lists, Email, Social, Pipeline, Goals, Analytics, Team, Settings
Each item has a Lucide icon and label
Active item highlighted with accent color
Sidebar collapsible to icon-only mode (persisted in user preferences)
Organization logo at top of sidebar (falls back to SalesBlock logo)
User avatar and name at bottom with sign-out option
Main content area fills remaining viewport width
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark mode / light mode toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want to toggle between light and dark themes so I can work comfortably.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme toggle in sidebar (sun/moon icon)
Dark mode uses dark background (#0a0a0a range), light text, muted borders
Light mode uses white/gray backgrounds, dark text
Theme preference saved to user's Supabase preferences jsonb
Respects system preference on first load (prefers-color-scheme)
All components support both themes via Tailwind dark: variant
No flash of wrong theme on page load
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: White-label logo upload
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want to upload my company logo so the app feels branded.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Organization: logo upload area (drag-drop or click to browse)
Accepts PNG, JPG, SVG (max 2MB)
Uploaded to Supabase Storage, URL saved to organizations.logo_url
Logo appears in sidebar top and sign-in page
Falls back to SalesBlock.io default logo if none set
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Contact and list database schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need contact, list, and junction tables so reps can manage prospect data.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration creates contacts table: id, org_id (FK), first_name, last_name, email, phone, company, title, source (enum: csv, salesforce, manual), custom_fields (jsonb), created_by (FK users), created_at, updated_at
SQL migration creates lists table: id, org_id (FK), name, description, filter_criteria (jsonb), owner_id (FK users), is_shared (boolean), created_at, updated_at
SQL migration creates list_contacts junction table: list_id (FK), contact_id (FK), added_at, position (int)
RLS: users see contacts in their org; list owners and shared-with users can see list contents
Indexes on org_id, email, company for fast filtering
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CSV contact import
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to import contacts from a CSV file so I can populate my contact database quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Lists page has Import CSV button that opens upload modal
Drag-drop or click-to-browse CSV file selection
Column mapping step: auto-detect common headers, allow manual remapping
Preview first 5 rows before import
Import creates contacts and optionally adds to a list
Duplicate detection by email with skip/update/create options
Progress indicator during import with summary on completion
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use Papa Parse for CSV handling. Max 10,000 contacts per import.

userStories.title: Manual contact creation
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to manually add a contact so I can track prospects not in my CSV.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Contact button on Contacts/Lists page
Form: first name, last name, email, phone, company, title, custom fields (key-value)
Email validation (format check)
Duplicate warning if email already exists in org
After creation, option to add to a list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: List builder with filters
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to create and save filtered contact lists so I can target specific segments.
acceptanceCriteria: Create List opens builder with name, description, and filter criteria
Filter by: company, title, source, date added, custom field values
Multiple filters combinable with AND logic
Live preview shows matching contact count as filters change
Save List persists filter criteria and current matching contacts
Auto-refresh toggle: when on, re-runs filter criteria on each view
Lists page shows all lists with contact count, owner, last refreshed date
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: List detail view with contact table
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to view and manage contacts within a list to prepare for salesblocks.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a list opens detail view with contact table
Table columns: name, company, title, email, phone, last activity date
Sortable columns (click header)
Searchable contacts within the list by name/email/company
Add to List and Remove from List actions
Start SalesBlock button to begin a session from this list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: SalesBlock and activity database schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need salesblock and activity tables to store session and outreach data.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration creates salesblocks table: id, org_id (FK), list_id (FK), user_id (FK), assigned_by (FK nullable), title, scheduled_start (timestamptz), scheduled_end (timestamptz), actual_start (timestamptz nullable), actual_end (timestamptz nullable), duration_minutes (int), status (enum: scheduled, in_progress, completed, cancelled), calendar_event_id (text nullable), notes (text), created_at

SQL migration creates activities table: id, org_id (FK), contact_id (FK), user_id (FK), salesblock_id (FK nullable), type (enum: call, email, social, meeting, note), outcome (enum: no_answer, voicemail, connect, conversation, meeting_booked, not_interested, follow_up, other), notes (text), duration_seconds (int nullable), created_at
RLS: users see their own salesblocks/activities; managers see team's
Indexes on user_id, list_id, scheduled_start, status
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create a salesblock form
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to create a salesblock from a list so I can schedule a focused outreach session.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SalesBlock button on Lists and SalesBlocks pages
Form: select list, title (auto-generated from list name + date), date/time, duration (15/25/30/45/60 min dropdown)
Creates salesblock record with status scheduled
Appears on SalesBlocks page and Home dashboard
Manager can create salesblocks assigned to team members (assigned_by populated)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Calendar preview placeholder — filled when calendar integration done.

userStories.title: SalesBlock session cockpit layout
userStories.description: As a rep, I want an active session view with contact queue, timer, and active contact detail.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Start on a scheduled salesblock opens session cockpit
Status changes to in_progress, actual_start recorded
Layout: contact queue on left (scrollable list), active contact detail on right
Timer bar at top showing elapsed time / total duration with progress bar
Active contact shows: name, company, title, phone (tel: link), email, last activity, notes
Next Contact button advances queue; Skip moves contact to end
Contact shows green checkmark in queue when at least one activity logged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Session cockpit activity logging
userStories.description: As a rep, I want quick-log buttons to record calls, emails, social touches, and notes during a salesblock.
acceptanceCriteria: Activity buttons in cockpit: Log Call, Log Email, Log Social, Log Note
Each opens quick-log modal with outcome dropdown and notes text area
Save auto-fills contact_id, salesblock_id, user_id
Call script panel: expandable/collapsible, shows assigned script content
Activity count updates in real-time on the contact queue
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: SalesBlock session completion summary
userStories.description: As a rep, I want a summary when my salesblock ends so I can see what I accomplished.
acceptanceCriteria: Timer expiry or End Session button triggers completion
Status changes to completed, actual_end recorded
Summary screen: total contacts worked, calls made, emails sent, social touches, meetings booked, total duration
Conversion ratios: contacts worked / total, calls to connects, connects to meetings
Save Notes text area for session-level observations
Back to Home button returns to dashboard

userStories.title: SalesBlocks list page
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to see all my salesblocks (upcoming, in-progress, completed) in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab filters: Upcoming, In Progress, Completed, All
Each card: title, list name, date/time, duration, status badge, contact count
Upcoming: Start button (if time is now/past), Edit, Cancel actions
Completed: shows summary stats inline (contacts worked, meetings booked)
Sortable by date
Manager view: toggle My SalesBlocks / Team SalesBlocks

userStories.title: Home dashboard
userStories.description: As a rep, I want a home screen showing today's salesblocks, recent activity, and progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Top section: Today's salesblock(s) with Start Block button for next scheduled
If no salesblocks today: Schedule a SalesBlock call-to-action
Activity feed: chronological recent activities with contact name, type icon, outcome, time

Goal progress indicators: calls made / target, meetings booked / target

userStories.title: Call scripts CRUD
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to create and manage call scripts for guidance during salesblocks.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration creates call_scripts table: id, org_id, name, content (text/markdown), owner_id (FK), is_shared (boolean), created_at, updated_at
Scripts page: list all scripts with name, owner, shared status
Create/Edit: name + rich text editor for script content
Delete with confirmation dialog
Shared scripts visible to team; personal only to owner
Scripts selectable when creating a salesblock

userStories.title: Email templates CRUD
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to create and manage email templates for consistent outreach.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration creates email_templates table: id, org_id, name, subject, body (text), owner_id (FK), is_shared (boolean), times_used (int default 0), reply_count (int default 0), created_at, updated_at
Templates page: list all with name, subject preview, owner, usage stats
Create/Edit: name, subject, body with variable placeholders ({{first_name}}, {{company}}, {{title}})
Preview mode shows template with sample data filled in
Delete with confirmation dialog
Shared templates visible to team members

userStories.title: Gmail OAuth connection
userStories.description: As a rep using Gmail, I want to connect my Google account so I can send emails from SalesBlock.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Integrations: Connect Gmail button
OAuth 2.0 flow with Google requesting gmail.send and gmail.readonly scopes
Refresh token stored securely in Supabase (encrypted column or vault)
Shows connected email address with Disconnect option
Connection status indicator in Email section

userStories.title: Outlook OAuth connection
userStories.description: As a rep using Outlook, I want to connect my Microsoft account so I can send emails from SalesBlock.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Integrations: Connect Outlook button
OAuth 2.0 flow with Microsoft Graph API requesting Mail.Send and Mail.Read scopes
Refresh token stored securely in Supabase
Shows connected email address with Disconnect option
Connection status indicator in Email section

userStories.title: Send one-to-one email from SalesBlock
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to send a personalized email to a contact without switching apps.
acceptanceCriteria: Email button on contact detail (in cockpit and contact list)
Compose modal: To (pre-filled), Subject, Body
Use Template dropdown populates from saved templates with variables auto-filled from contact data
Send via connected Gmail API or Outlook Graph API (sends as rep's email)
If no email connected: prompt to connect in Settings
After send: auto-logs email activity on contact with subject and timestamp

userStories.title: Email reply tracking background job
userStories.description: As a rep, I want replies to my emails detected automatically so I can follow up and track template effectiveness.
acceptanceCriteria: Supabase Edge Function polls Gmail/Outlook inbox for replies to sent emails (every 5 min cron)
Matches replies to original sent email by thread ID / conversation ID
Email activity on contact timeline shows sent and replied timestamps
No open tracking (no tracking pixels — preserves deliverability)
Email templates page shows aggregate reply_count per template
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Log social activity (manual)
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to manually log LinkedIn and social outreach activities.
acceptanceCriteria: Log Social button in session cockpit and on contact detail page
Modal: platform dropdown (LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Other), activity type (Connection Request, Message Sent, InMail, Comment, Post Engagement), notes, timestamp
Creates activity record with type=social
Appears in contact timeline alongside calls and emails
Social page: list all social activities with filters by platform, type, date range

userStories.description: As a rep using Google Calendar, I want to connect my calendar so salesblocks sync as events.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Integrations: Connect Google Calendar button
OAuth 2.0 flow requesting calendar.events scope
Refresh token stored securely

userStories.description: As a rep using Outlook, I want to connect my Microsoft calendar so salesblocks sync as events.

userStories.title: Sync salesblocks to calendar
userStories.description: As a rep, I want salesblocks to appear on my calendar so my focus time is protected.
acceptanceCriteria: When salesblock created with scheduled time: creates calendar event via connected API
Event title: SalesBlock: [session title], description: list name, contact count, duration
calendar_event_id stored on salesblock record
When rescheduled: updates calendar event
When cancelled: deletes calendar event
Works with whichever calendar is connected (Google or Outlook)

userStories.title: Insert availability and book meetings
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to share availability and book meetings from within SalesBlock.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert Availability button in email compose reads calendar for free/busy and generates formatted time slots
Time slots inserted into email body as selectable text
Book Meeting button on contact detail creates calendar event with contact as attendee
Meeting form: title, date/time, duration, description, attendee email
Created meeting auto-logged as activity (type=meeting) on the contact

userStories.title: Salesforce OAuth connection
userStories.description: As a rep with Salesforce, I want to connect my SF account to sync contacts and push activities.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Integrations: Connect Salesforce button
OAuth 2.0 Connected App flow (authorization code grant)
Access and refresh tokens stored securely
Shows connected status with org name and Disconnect option

userStories.title: Import contacts from Salesforce
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to import contacts from Salesforce so I don't re-enter data.
acceptanceCriteria: Lists page: Import from Salesforce button (visible only when SF connected)
Object selection: Leads, Contacts, or Accounts (radio buttons)
Filter by owner (default: current user), date range
Preview matching records with count before import
Maps SF fields to SalesBlock contact fields (Name, Email, Phone, Company, Title)
Stores salesforce_id on contact record for future sync
Duplicate detection by email
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Push activities to Salesforce
userStories.description: As a rep, I want my SalesBlock activities to sync back to Salesforce.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Salesforce: toggle Auto-push activities on/off
When on: after each activity logged, create Task record in SF on matching Lead/Contact
Maps activity type to SF Task Subject, notes to Description, outcome to Status
Sync happens asynchronously (queue-based, does not block UI)
Sync status indicator per activity: pending, synced, failed (with retry)
Manual Sync Now button for pending activities
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pipeline stages and deals schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pipeline_stages and deals tables for the Kanban pipeline view.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration creates pipeline_stages table: id, org_id (FK), name, position (int), color (text), probability (int default 0), is_default (boolean), created_at
Default stages seeded: Prospect, Qualified, Proposal, Negotiation, Closed Won, Closed Lost

SQL migration creates deals table: id, org_id (FK), contact_id (FK), user_id (FK), stage_id (FK), title, value (decimal), currency (text default USD), close_date (date nullable), custom_fields (jsonb), notes (text), created_at, updated_at
RLS: users see deals in their org; can edit their own deals
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pipeline Kanban board
userStories.description: As a rep, I want a visual pipeline board to track deals through stages.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline page shows Kanban board with columns for each stage
Deal cards show: title, contact name, value (formatted currency), close date, days in stage
Drag-and-drop to move deals between stages (updates stage_id)
Add Deal button: title, contact (search/select), value, close date, stage
Click deal card to open detail view with all fields and activity log
Column header shows deal count and total value per stage
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Basic revenue forecasting
userStories.description: As a manager, I want basic revenue forecasting based on pipeline data.
acceptanceCriteria: Forecast bar below Kanban showing weighted pipeline value
Each stage has configurable probability % (in Settings > Pipeline)
Forecast = sum of (deal.value * stage.probability) for all open deals
Shows: total pipeline value, weighted forecast, deals closing this month
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Goal setting and tracking
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to set and track daily/weekly/monthly goals.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration creates goals table: id, org_id (FK), user_id (FK), metric (enum: calls, emails, social_touches, meetings_booked, pipeline_value, custom), target_value (int), period (enum: daily, weekly, monthly), custom_metric_name (text nullable), created_at
Goals page: list current goals with progress bars
Add Goal form: metric dropdown, target number, period
Progress auto-calculated by counting activities of that type in the current period
Progress bar with percentage, color shifts green as approaching target
Goals visible on Home dashboard as compact progress indicators
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Unified contact activity timeline
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to see all activities for a contact in one chronological timeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact detail page shows chronological activity timeline
Each entry: type icon, outcome badge, notes preview, timestamp
Filter by activity type

Timeline visible in contact detail page and salesblock session cockpit
Most recent activity at top (reverse chronological)

userStories.title: Analytics dashboard — activity metrics
userStories.description: As a rep, I want to see my activity metrics and trends.

Comparison to previous period (e.g., this week vs last week)
Ratios: call-to-connect %, connect-to-meeting %, emails-to-reply %

userStories.title: Custom KPI builder
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create custom KPIs to track metrics specific to my workflow.

Custom KPIs displayed as cards on Analytics page alongside built-in metrics

userStories.title: Team dashboard (manager view)

userStories.title: Stripe pricing page and checkout

userStories.title: Stripe webhook handling and subscription management

userStories.title: Team invitation flow

userStories.title: Organization hierarchy management
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want to configure divisions and teams so data access reflects our structure.

Create/rename/delete divisions (optional grouping layer)

userStories.title: Settings page shell with tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I want a settings page to manage my profile, integrations, and preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page with tabs: Profile, Organization, Team, Integrations, Pipeline, Billing
Profile tab: display name, email (read-only), password change, preferences (theme, sidebar collapsed default)
Organization tab: name, logo upload (US-007), hierarchy management (US-046)
Team tab: invite members (US-045), list current members with roles
Integrations tab: Gmail, Outlook, Google Calendar, Outlook Calendar, Salesforce — each with connect/disconnect
Pipeline tab: manage stages (add, rename, reorder, set probability %, delete)
Billing tab: subscription management (US-044)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This ties together many previous stories into the Settings UI.
